response.justification: Candidate has 15+ years of solid software engineering experience but the profile is overwhelmingly web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) with no demonstrated work on embedded firmware, LAN-switch products, or the specific security technologies required (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal). Critically, the role requires native-level Japanese proficiency, which the resume does not list at all (only English, Romanian, basic German). C experience is limited to a short stint at National Instruments a decade ago, and there is no evidence of Linux kernel modification, chip-vendor SDKs, or L2/L3 networking expertise.
strengths: Long track record (15+ years) of professional software development across multiple domains
Some C/C++ exposure via National Instruments LabWindows/CVI work
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testability, open-source contributions
concerns: No native-level Japanese proficiency listed (hard requirement)
No embedded firmware, LAN-switch, or networking-security experience (MACsec, 802.1X, NETCONF, SNMP)
C in UNIX and Linux kernel modification experience is minimal and dated

key_factors.factor: Insufficient recent C and Linux kernel work
